name: squat to overhead reach with twist
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you come up from the squat, raise your arms overhead and twist your torso to one side.
Return to the starting position and repeat the squat, this time twisting your torso to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides with each squat repetition.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back. As you come up from the squat, raise your arms overhead and twist your torso to one side. Return to the starting position and repeat the squat, this time twisting your torso to the opposite side. Continue alternating sides with each squat repetition.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Overhead positions can stress shoulder and elbow joints. Reduce ROM and load as needed.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Single-leg or balance-intensive variations increase fall risk. Use support or a seated alternative.
Avoid pushing into end-range positions. Prioritize midrange control and joint stability over depth.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
